Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a digital marketing or web development service offered by Mix Aura, which are showcased through abstract 3D structures in the application's UI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Service entity.

name.description: The name of the service, e.g., 'Digital Marketing' or 'Web Development'.

description.description: A detailed description of what the service entails, to provide context and information to users.

iconVisualizationAssetPath.description: URI to a 3D model or asset used for the service's abstract visualization in the UI.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a portfolio project or case study by Mix Aura, displayed as glowing holographic portals in the UI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

title.description: The title of the project or case study.

clientName.description: The name of the client for whom the project was completed.

shortDescription.description: A brief, high-level overview of the project, suitable for display on the holographic portal.

longDescription.description: Detailed description and insights into the project, serving as the full-screen cinematic case study content.

startDate.description: The start date of the project.

endDate.description: The completion date of the project. This field is optional if the project is ongoing.

technologiesUsed.description: A list of key technologies, tools, or platforms utilized in the project.

projectUrl.description: Optional URL to the live version or an external showcase of the project.

thumbnailAssetPath.description: URI to the asset (e.g., image, 3D model) used for the holographic portal thumbnail in the portfolio display.

fullScreenAssetPaths.description: An array of URIs to images or videos displayed in the full-screen cinematic view of the project.

serviceIds.description: References to Service entities this project is associated with. (Relationship: Service N:N Project)

ContactSubmission.title: ContactSubmission
ContactSubmission.description: Stores messages submitted through the futuristic contact form, acting as a digital command center for user inquiries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactSubmission entity.

name.description: The name of the person submitting the contact form.

email.description: The email address of the sender, used for replies.

company.description: The company name of the sender (optional).

message.description: The detailed message or inquiry from the sender.

submissionDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the contact form was submitted.

status.description: The current processing status of the submission (e.g., 'new', 'read', 'replied', 'archived').

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection for Mix Aura's services. All services are visible to all users. Administrative users have write access.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific service.

definition.description: Publicly accessible collection for Mix Aura's portfolio projects. All projects are visible to all users. Administrative users have write access. Includes references to Service entities via 'serviceIds' array.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific project.

definition.description: Collection for contact form submissions. Anonymous users can create new submissions. Only authenticated administrative users (checked via '/roles_admin/{uid}') can read, update, or delete submissions.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific contact submission.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to designate administrative users. The mere existence of a document at '/roles_admin/{uid}' grants 'admin' permissions for that user. This collection is for internal role management and security rule checks.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique user ID (UID) of an administrative user.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes clarity, security, and scalability by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

For `Service` and `Project` entities, a simple top-level collection structure (`/services/{serviceId}` and `/projects/{projectId}`) is chosen. This design explicitly supports public read access, making it straightforward to implement security rules that permit all users to view these showcases. This approach inherently achieves Authorization Independence as access is not derived from any parent document or associated user context; all documents in these collections have the same public read posture. For write operations (create, update, delete), these collections will be restricted to authenticated administrative users.

For `ContactSubmission` entities, a dedicated top-level collection (`/contactSubmissions/{submissionId}`) is used. This segregates private, sensitive data from public content. Authorization for `ContactSubmission` follows a hybrid model: public users are permitted to *create* new submissions, but only authenticated administrative users can *read*, *update*, or *delete* them. This ensures data privacy and controlled management. To achieve Authorization Independence for read/update/delete operations, an admin role is established through a separate, top-level collection: `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. Access to `contactSubmissions` for administrative tasks is granted based solely on the *existence* of a document for `request.auth.uid` within the `/roles_admin` collection. This avoids `get()` calls on hierarchical parent documents or complex attribute checks within the `ContactSubmission` document itself, simplifying security rules and enabling atomic operations.

Regarding **QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**:

*   **`/services` and `/projects`**: These collections are designed for entirely public consumption. Security rules will simply allow `read` access for all users, meaning any `list` query will return all documents, which perfectly aligns with the application's requirement to showcase all services and projects. No filtering is required or expected by the rules.
*   **`/contactSubmissions`**: For non-admin users, `list` operations on this collection will be explicitly denied by the security rules, as only admin users have read access. For admin users, `list` operations will return all submissions, as they are authorized to view all of them. This ensures that rules explicitly define who can `list` and prevents unauthorized retrieval of a partial or full dataset, fully adhering to QAPs. The `/roles_admin` collection serves as a direct authorization check for administrative permissions, ensuring that `list` calls are only successful when the user is an administrator.
